meaning: n.夜间活动,夜袭,(尤指在夜间)从事第二职业
examples: If you're smart, you'll loop in your boss before doing any moonlighting.
Moonlighting traditionally has meant juggling two hourly jobs, or an hourly job plus freelancing.
Last fall, he started moonlighting as a small-business consultant and personal finance counselor.
Across developed economies, the recession has brought in a new wave of moonlighting Amway reps.
Moonlighting on other jobs and travel time from distant cities should be included in work-hour limits.
